label: Ingrown toenail without abscess or cellulitis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of ingrown toenail.
No abscess, cellulitis, diabetic foot concern, poor circulation concern, nail procedure aftercare pathway, or systemic illness.
exclusion_criteria: Abscess, cellulitis, pus, fever, or systemic illness.
Diabetes, poor circulation, immunocompromised host, neuropathy, or diabetic foot pathway.
Recent nail procedure or specialist-directed podiatry plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or podiatry if home care is not helping or if this keeps coming back.

source_audit.notes: NHS supports ingrown-toenail framing, pain/redness/swelling symptoms, warm salty-water soaks, keeping the foot dry, wide footwear, label-safe pain control, and escalation for pus, feverish symptoms, failed home care, diabetes, antibiotics, or podiatry treatment. Autonomous Phase 1501-1550 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed ingrown toenail without abscess, cellulitis, pus, diabetic foot risk, poor circulation, immunocompromised-host pathway, or specialist procedure aftercare.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. Autonomous Phase 1501-1550 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed ingrown toenail without abscess, cellulitis, pus, diabetic foot risk, poor circulation, immunocompromised-host pathway, or specialist procedure aftercare.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of ingrown toenail
no abscess, cellulitis, pus, or spreading infection documented
no diabetes, poor circulation, neuropathy, immunocompromised-host, systemic illness, or procedure aftercare pathway
